review_questions: Does CP00-151 cover exactly the 150 planned Risk C failure continuation and Hermes evidence units in order?
Can any CP00-151 failure row evaluate taxonomy, execute recovery, emit receipts, write fixtures, execute tests, or expose hidden internals?
Can any CP00-151 evidence row execute Hermes commands, emit receipts, materialize evidence templates, send Claude prompts, record human markers, or write audit/product state?
Are H03/C03 packet descriptors and PASS/PASS_WITH_FINDINGS/BLOCK semantics reference-only and non-authoritative?
Does CP00-151 hand off cleanly from CP00-150 to CP00-152 without implementing LDIP or splitting HRX?

parsed_review.summary: CP00-151 is a Risk C no-write descriptor pack that binds 150 planned units (RP03.P07.M06.S09-RP03.P08.M04.S19) as failure-continuation and Hermes-evidence-packet reference rows. I independently recomputed the catalog: the 10 micro-phase slices (P07.M06 S09-S22, P07.M07/M08 full, P07.M09 1-20, P07.M10 1-11, P08.M00 1-4, M01/M02 1-8, M03 full, M04 1-19) sum to exactly 150, first unit = RP03.P07.M06.S09, last = RP03.P08.M04.S19, all IDs unique and ordered. Deliverable counts (failure_recovery 51, implementation 24, hermes_evidence 48, fixture 4, test 10, security_audit 8, claude_review 5 = 150) and the ten evidence_mode counts match the test assertions exactly. The schema bump to v0.18, current_pack pointer (CP00-151, Risk C, 150 units, next CP00-152 / RP03.P08.M04.S20), README, contract additions, and validator updates are mutually consistent. All validator case IDs (ambiguous_rule, blocked_claim, hermes_failure, command_matrix, pass_semantics, regression) resolve to real rows, so no unknown-case throw. No-write enforcement is robust: freezeNoWriteResult applies explicit false overrides after the spread, so no caller can inject a truthy write flag; cross-tenant, forbidden-payload (name-only denylist, no values), LDIP, and HRX attestations are uniformly negative and double-checked by the .mjs validator. C03 review questions are all satisfied for a no-write pack (no event edit/delete, no tenant inference, no hold/retention bypass, no payload leakage, Hermes/Claude artifacts non-authoritative). node --check, 78 CP00-151 tests, and rp03:audit:validate all pass. No P0/P1/P2 blockers; only non-blocking P3 observations.

findings.title: Descriptor-only scope: no runtime failure-handling is implemented
findings.details: Consistent with the production_ready_flag 'audit_compliance_failure_evidence_continuation_verified', this pack binds reference descriptors only. It does not implement runtime failure recovery, taxonomy evaluation, blocked-claim/Hermes-evidence emission, fixture materialization, or Claude execution (all such attestations are false). The 'pass' recommendation attests that the descriptor-binding gate passed, not that audit failure-handling runtime is production-ready.
findings.recommendation: Ensure the human approver understands the gate semantics: CP00-151 verifies the continuation/evidence catalog binding, with actual failure-recovery and Hermes/Claude runtime deferred to later non-descriptor implementation packs.

findings.title: Hermes evidence packet is a non-authoritative template (unconditional 'passed' + manifest ref not produced here)
findings.details: createAuditComplianceCp151HermesEvidencePacket maps any caller-supplied command to status 'passed' without executing it (executes_hermes_command/emits_hermes_failure_evidence are false), and evidence_refs lists 'docs/closeout-packs/cp00-151/manifest.json', which is not created in this change. The test and validator tolerate the missing manifest via try/catch fallback to the closeout plan, so validation is unaffected, but the packet must never be consumed as real H03 evidence.
findings.recommendation: Keep treating this packet strictly as a reference template; if the closeout convention expects docs/closeout-packs/cp00-151/manifest.json, generate it during closeout so the evidence_refs entry resolves rather than relying on the fallback path.

findings.title: Coverage verified at unit-ID level only; per-unit semantic labels are author-asserted
findings.details: validateAuditComplianceCp151Coverage and the .mjs validator compare covered unit IDs (order/count/uniqueness) against the closeout plan, which is the correct binding contract. However, the human-readable titles and deliverable_types are assigned by template index within the catalog and are not cross-validated against the ledger's actual sub-unit semantics; only the SNN identifiers are checked. This is inherent to the established descriptor pattern and low risk, but it bounds the strength of the 'coverage verified' claim to identifiers, not meaning.
findings.recommendation: No change required for this gate; note the limitation when later implementation packs consume these descriptors, so that title/deliverable_type drift from the ledger is caught at implementation time.
